prompt:
Two complaints about the current ETRO essay. The article abuses bold font.
There's also not a clear separation between sections, somehow, or the
hierarchy not visually clean. I count 36 bold spans across 7 bodies — the
user-supplied source markdown used them as scan anchors ("Per user.",
"Scaffolding.", etc.). Fix it.
response:
I now have everything needed for a complete diagnosis. The picture is fully clear. Let me deliver the structured response. --- ## Diagnosis There are **two distinct problems** with different root causes and different fix layers. --- ### Problem 1: Bold Abuse (Content + CMS data layer) **Anti-pattern: CAP-030** — *Inline Bold Imported From Source Markdown* The user-supplied ETRO markdown used `**bold**` as scan-anchor typography — shorthand labels like `**Per user.**`, `**Scaffolding.**`, `**Full ETRO slows adoption.**` — placed at the start of paragraphs to function like subheadings. When CFB-035 materialized those blocks into Lexical richText via `/api/update-etro`, those `**...**` spans were faithfully preserved as `FORMAT_BOLD` text nodes (Lexical format bitmask `1`). The rendered `` tags produce weight-700 text sitting inside body copy, which is optically the same weight as a semibold (`600`) H2 section heading. The contrast that creates heading hierarchy collapses — the eye cannot distinguish "heading of this section" from "bold word in this paragraph." **Cross-category dimension:** This is simultaneously a content violation (`voice-style.md §13.1`: bold reserved for headings and impact metrics only) and a design violation (FB-143: body-weight bold reads as a peer of the H2 heading, collapsing the section rhythm). --- ### Problem 2: Section Hierarchy Not Visually Clean (CSS / rendering layer) **Anti-pattern: EAP-123** — *Missing `prose-paragraph-spacing` on HTML prose container* The essay section body containers need `@include prose-paragraph-spacing` applied so that `p + p` spacing is correct and each paragraph reads as a discrete beat. Without it, CSS resets strip all `

` margins and paragraphs run flush — the visual breathing room that signals "paragraph boundary" is absent. This is independent of bold, but together they compound: no inter-paragraph spacing + in-paragraph bold both collapse the same vertical hierarchy, making the whole section feel like undifferentiated text mass. The section heading itself (`heading-2`, semibold 600) floats without contrast above body content that has its own internal weight-700 punctuation. --- ### Root Causes | Problem | Root Cause | Anti-pattern | |---|---|---| | 36 bold spans in body copy | CFB-035's materialization loop preserved raw `**...**` from user markdown as Lexical `FORMAT_BOLD` nodes instead of stripping them at ingestion | **CAP-030** | | Section hierarchy collapse | Body `` at 700 is heavier than the H2's semibold 600 — the heading is visually outgunned by its own children | **CAP-030** (side effect: typography hierarchy inversion) | | Flat section rhythm (secondary) | `@include prose-paragraph-spacing` missing or insufficient on `.sectionBody` in essay layout, causing flush paragraph stacking | **EAP-123** | --- ### Fix The fix has two independent parts: **CMS data** and **CSS**. The CMS fix is the primary one. **Note:** The knowledge graph shows this fix was previously applied on 2026-04-17 and confirmed via Lexical tree walk. The most likely explanation for regression is a Payload DB reset or a re-seed that re-introduced the old block data. The fix must be re-applied. --- #### Part 1 — Strip all FORMAT_BOLD nodes from the ETRO richText bodies Find `src/app/api/update-etro/route.ts` (check `src/app/(frontend)/api/` or wherever the seeding routes live in your app structure). The transformation rule for every `bodyMarkdown` field: **Rule: Any `**text**` span in the source markdown must be converted before producing the Lexical node tree — never passed through as `FORMAT_BOLD`.** ``` Conversion table for scan-anchor bolds: "**Per user.**" → paragraph start: "Per user:" "**Scaffolding.**" → paragraph start: "Scaffolding:" "**Structure.**" → paragraph start: "Structure:" "**Migration.**" → paragraph start: "Migration:" "**Full ETRO slows adoption.**" → paragraph start: "Full ETRO slows adoption:" (any other **label.** ) → strip the bold markers; convert trailing period to colon if it's a structural label Sentence-level emphasis bolds: convert to plain text (let the prose carry the weight) Tonal emphasis words: convert to *italic* if emphasis is needed, never bold Rhetorical questions ("**What role does trust play here?...**"): strip the bold — the question marks carry the emphasis ``` **In the Lexical JSON builder**, ensure every text node in `children` arrays has `format: 0` (not `format: 1`). The check: ```ts // In your markdownToLexical helper or wherever richText is built: // After building the text node array, walk and zero out any FORMAT_BOLD: function stripBold(nodes: LexicalNode[]): LexicalNode[] { return nodes.map(node => { if (node.type === 'text' && (node.format & 1)) { return { ...node, format: node.format & ~1 }; // clear bit 0 (bold) } if (node.children) { return { ...node, children: stripBold(node.children) }; } return node; }); } ``` Apply `stripBold()` to every `richText` root before the POST to Payload. This produces `format: 0` across all 7 section bodies. **After updating the route source, you MUST call the endpoint** (EAP-087 — the canonical trap for this workflow): ```bash curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/update-etro # Verify the response confirms action: "updated" and block count is still 13 ``` Then verify in the Payload admin that no `` tags appear in the rendered HTML of any section body. --- #### Part 2 — Restore section visual separation (CSS) In the essay layout SCSS — `src/app/(frontend)/work/[slug]/page.module.scss` (or wherever `.layoutEssay` / `.contentEssay` / `.sectionBody` classes live): **Check 1: `prose-paragraph-spacing` mixin is present on `.sectionBody`** ```scss // src/app/(frontend)/work/[slug]/page.module.scss .sectionBody { @include prose-paragraph-spacing; // ← must exist; adds p + p { margin-top: $portfolio-spacer-1-5x } @include body-lg; // editorial body for essay format } ``` If it's missing, add it. The `prose-paragraph-spacing` mixin lives in `src/styles/mixins/_layout.scss`. **Check 2: Section-to-section gap is sufficient** For the essay format, sections need more vertical air than case study image blocks. Confirm the `.section` gap in essay layout context: ```scss .layoutEssay .section + .section { margin-top: $portfolio-spacer-4x; // minimum for essay; 6x if sections feel cramped } ``` **Check 3: H2 heading weight contrast vs. body** The section heading should be `heading-2` (semibold 600). The body should be `body-lg` (regular 400). With zero bold nodes in the body, the 200-weight delta between heading (600) and body (400) is now sufficient contrast. No additional CSS change is needed — it was the in-body bold nodes (700) that were the confounders. --- ### Principle **Voice-style.md §13.1** states this explicitly: > *Bold: only for section headings and impact metrics. Never in body text for emphasis.* This is the single rule that governs both complaints. The section hierarchy complaint is not a spacing problem at its root — it is a **weight-hierarchy inversion problem**. When body copy contains `font-weight: 700` (via ``), and section headings are `font-weight: 600` (semibold), the heading is the *lightest* thing on the page. Every bold scan anchor reads as a mini-heading, fracturing the section into sub-sub-sections the design system never intended. Removing the bold nodes restores the correct hierarchy with zero CSS changes: heading (600) > body (400). The paragraph spacing fix (EAP-123) addresses the secondary rhythm problem, but the primary fix is the Lexical tree. **For future source ingestion (CAP-030 prevention):** Any user-supplied markdown flowing into a seeding route must be pre-processed through a bold-strip pass before the Lexical node is built. Never trust that user markdown formatting conventions align with the portfolio's typographic contract. The strip should happen at the **data layer** (in `update-etro/route.ts`), not at the **rendering layer**, so that the source of truth in Payload is already clean.
